OpenRemediationsHint: Tasks requiring action across all vendors

Audit.title: Audit Trail
Audit.description: Privacy-first forensic event history. NIS2 · DORA · EU AI Act · ISO 27001 · SOC2 · GDPR compliant.
Audit.legendPrefix: Privacy-first forensic log — IP truncated (GDPR Rec. 30), hash-chained (NIS2/DORA Art. 9), AI identity tracked (EU AI Act Art. 12/14).

privacy.gdprArt5Minimization: PII redacted per GDPR Art. 5(1)(c): Data minimization

DashboardDesc: Third-party risk posture overview for NIS2-aligned oversight.
SupplyChainRiskScoreDesc: Aggregated from vendor compliance scores (0–100). Higher reflects stronger collective posture.
TrustComplianceDesc: Venshield helps security officers evidence due diligence across the digital supply chain. Figures on this page are read from your Venshield database.
RiskColoring: Risk coloring: red below 40, amber 40–70, green above 70.
AssessmentWorkspace: Assessment workspace pairs the NIS2 questionnaire with evidence review.
InviteNewVendors: Invite new vendors from the vendor list; changes persist to the database.
RiskPostureOverviewDesc: Mission-control view of category coverage, vendor concentration, and AI-generated executive signals across your supply chain.
RiskPostureOverviewTooltip: Aggregated compliance scores across all your assessed vendors. Higher scores indicate stronger overall NIS2/DORA posture.
CategoryComplianceRadarDesc: Normalized across all vendors and category questions. Missing answers count as uncovered controls.
CategoryComplianceRadarTooltip: Average compliance per NIS2 control category. Missing answers reduce category scores.
CategoryComplianceRadarHoverHint: Hover over a data point to view details
VendorsByRiskLevelDesc: Current vendor distribution derived from live assessment scores and reconciled risk levels.
VendorsByRiskLevelTooltip: Vendor count by risk tier: High (below 40), Moderate (40–70), Low (above 70).
AIExecutiveSummaryDesc: LLM analysis of the current vendor set using live assessment and answer data.
AIReportCTA: AI draft available — Review in Reports
BiggestSystemicRisk: Biggest systemic risk in your supply chain is
CategoryRadarEmptyDesc: Complete vendor assessments to see coverage by NIS2 control category.
VendorsByRiskEmptyDesc: Assess vendors to see risk distribution across your supply chain.
ComplianceTrustWidgetDesc: Safety-certificate snapshot proving Venshield AI governance, human oversight, and immutable audit traceability.
AITransparencyTooltip: Counts every AI-generated assessment output, supporting EU AI Act Article 13 transparency and traceability obligations.
HumanOversightTooltip: Percentage of AI suggestions reviewed and accepted or modified by a human operator, satisfying EU AI Act Article 14 human oversight requirements.
SystemIntegrityTooltip: Percentage of audit log entries verified via cryptographic hash chain. A value below 100% indicates potential tampering and must be investigated under NIS2 Article 21 audit requirements.
DownloadForensicAuditHint: Export all AI interactions as a quick integrity packet for legal and executive review.
DownloadForensicAuditFailed: Forensic audit summary could not be downloaded.

OrganizationSettingsDesc: Configure platform-wide settings for your organisation.
YourAccountDesc: Manage your personal password and multi-factor authentication.
SettingsTabGeneralDesc: Configure AI provider settings and data residency for your organisation.
SettingsTabSecurityDesc: Manage authentication settings including SSO, MFA policy, and API access.
SettingsTabIntegrationsDesc: Connect external services: email delivery, SSO, webhook endpoints, and API access.
SettingsTabComplianceDesc: Customise assessment questionnaires, question sets, and compliance framework libraries.
MailSettingsDesc: Manage SMTP and API providers for outgoing emails.
VerifyDataResidency: Verify where your vendor data is actively processed during AI assessments.
CurrentEnvironmentRouting: Current environment routing for inference telemetry.
ResidencyMessage: Your active workspace is configured to route vendor evidence strictly to the designated endpoint above.
LocalResidencyNote: Air-gapped internal telemetry guarantees zero EU-egress.
EUResidencyNote: Enterprise agreements ensure strict EU data residency alignment via Mistral platforms.
SelectAIProvider: Select your preferred AI provider for NIS2 assessments. Data processing location affects compliance.
MistralApiKeyHint: Routes AI analysis to Mistral's EU cloud. Leave blank to keep the existing key.
KeyAlreadyConfigured: Leave blank to keep the existing key
noAiModeDesc: When active, all AI-powered features are disabled. Manual workflows remain fully functional.

welcome.title: Welcome to the Venshield Assessment Portal
welcome.subtitle: Securing the supply chain for Adventure Huso.
welcome.purposeBody1: The European Union's NIS2 Directive requires organizations to ensure the security of their supply chains. As a valued partner of Adventure Huso, we invite you to complete this security assessment.
welcome.purposeBody2: This process helps us maintain a high security standard and fulfill our regulatory obligations. You can either upload existing security documentation for AI-assisted analysis or complete the questionnaire manually.
welcome.fastPathBody: Upload your ISO 27001, SOC2, or policy PDF and let AI suggest your answers.
welcome.directPathBody: Answer 20 targeted security questions directly in our interactive wizard.
welcome.footer: Secure isolated environment - Powered by Venshield Sovereign Compliance Engine

fastPath.title: The fast way
fastPath.description: Upload your security policy or certification (ISO 27001, SOC2). Our AI will analyze your documents to pre-fill the questionnaire.

noAiTraining.title: No AI training
noAiTraining.body: Your documents are never used to train or improve the underlying AI models.

euHosted.title: EU-hosted (Germany)
euHosted.body: All analysis is processed on isolated, sovereign servers in Frankfurt (eu-central-1).

stateless.title: Stateless analysis
stateless.body: Documents exist only in memory during analysis. Nothing is stored permanently; files are discarded immediately after.

tls.title: TLS 1.3 encryption
tls.body: Your data is encrypted in transit using high industry standards.

externalAssessment.importantMessage: Important: AI-suggested answers appear as highlights. You must verify and confirm them in the questionnaire to complete your submission.

errors.unexpectedSave: Unexpected error while saving. Please retry.

questionnaireWizard.fallbackFindings: Based on your documents, this requirement appears to be met.
questionnaireWizard.justificationPlaceholder: Explain why this status applies. You can edit AI-generated reasoning before saving.
questionnaireWizard.justificationHint: This text is editable and will be sanitized on save.

q1.text: Does the vendor maintain a documented information security policy approved by management?
q1.guidance: Policy should cover roles, acceptable use, and review cadence.

q2.text: Is there a designated security officer or equivalent accountable for cybersecurity?

q3.text: Are cybersecurity risks assessed at least annually and after major changes?

q4.text: Is multi-factor authentication enforced for all administrative and remote access?

q5.text: Are least-privilege and role-based access controls applied to production systems?

q6.text: Is access reviewed periodically and revoked promptly on role change or offboarding?

q7.text: Is personal and sensitive data classified, with handling rules documented?

q8.text: Are data processing agreements and subprocessors documented and kept current?

q9.text: Is data encrypted in transit and at rest using industry-accepted algorithms?

q10.text: Are cryptographic keys generated, stored, and rotated according to a defined process?

q11.text: Is use of customer-managed keys or HSMs supported where required?

q12.text: Are security logs collected centrally with retention aligned to regulatory needs?

q13.text: Is 24/7 monitoring in place for critical services with defined escalation paths?

q14.text: Are vulnerability management and patch SLAs defined and evidenced?

q15.text: Does the vendor maintain an incident response plan with customer notification clauses?

q16.text: Are incident exercises or tabletop tests performed and improvements tracked?

q17.text: Are business continuity and disaster recovery plans tested with defined RTO/RPO?

q18.text: Is secure SDLC practiced (threat modeling, code review, dependency scanning)?

q19.text: Are third-party components and open-source licenses tracked and monitored?

q20.text: Are penetration tests or independent security assessments performed regularly?

sessionExpired.title: Session expired

nis2.description: EU Network and Information Security directive — core cybersecurity governance and incident response controls.

dora.name: DORA
dora.description: Digital Operational Resilience Act — ICT risk management for financial sector entities.

iso27001.name: ISO 27001
iso27001.description: International standard for information security management systems (ISMS).

soc2_type2.name: SOC 2 Type II
soc2_type2.description: Trust Services Criteria — Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, and Privacy.

hipaa.name: HIPAA
hipaa.description: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — PHI protection and breach notification.

nist_csf.name: NIST CSF
nist_csf.description: Cybersecurity Framework — Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover functions.

cis_controls_v8.name: CIS Controls v8
cis_controls_v8.description: 18 prioritized safeguard groups for enterprise cyber defense.

0.0: Has the organization designated a management body responsible for cybersecurity risk oversight?
0.1: Are cybersecurity risks formally assessed and documented at least annually?
0.2: Describe the organization's cybersecurity governance framework and reporting lines.
0.3: How frequently does senior management review cybersecurity risk reports?

1.0: Is there a documented incident response plan that covers detection, containment, and recovery?
1.1: Are significant incidents reported to the relevant national CSIRT within 24 hours?
1.2: Describe the incident classification criteria used to determine reportable incidents.
1.3: What is the average time to detect a security incident in your environment?
1.4: Are incident response procedures tested at least annually?

2.0: Are cybersecurity requirements included in contracts with critical suppliers?
2.1: Is there a process to assess the cybersecurity posture of third-party vendors before onboarding?
2.2: Describe the vendor risk assessment methodology applied to critical service providers.
2.3: How often are critical suppliers reassessed for cybersecurity compliance?

3.0: Are multi-factor authentication mechanisms deployed for privileged accounts?
3.1: Is encryption applied to sensitive data both at rest and in transit?
3.2: Describe the organization's patch management process and average deployment timeline.
3.3: Are network segmentation controls in place to isolate critical systems?
3.4: What percentage of endpoints have up-to-date anti-malware protection?

0.0: Has the organization established a comprehensive ICT risk management framework approved by the management body?
0.1: Are ICT risks identified, assessed, and documented in a risk register?
0.2: Describe the process for continuously monitoring and updating ICT risk assessments.
0.3: How frequently is the ICT risk management framework reviewed and updated?

1.0: Are threat-led penetration tests (TLPT) conducted at least every three years for critical systems?
1.1: Is there a structured testing program covering vulnerability assessments, scenario-based tests, and recovery drills?
1.2: Describe the scope and methodology of the most recent resilience testing exercise.
1.3: Are test results reported to senior management and incorporated into remediation plans?
1.4: What is the average remediation time for critical vulnerabilities identified during testing?

2.0: Is there a formal register of all ICT third-party service providers maintained and regularly updated?
2.1: Are contractual arrangements with critical ICT providers aligned with DORA requirements (exit strategies, data access, audit rights)?
2.2: Describe the due diligence process applied before engaging a critical ICT third-party provider.
2.3: Are concentration risks related to ICT service providers actively monitored and mitigated?

3.0: Is there a documented incident classification framework aligned with DORA's criteria (materiality thresholds)?
3.1: Are major ICT-related incidents reported to competent authorities within required timeframes?
3.2: Describe the escalation process for classifying and reporting material ICT incidents.
3.3: How many major ICT-related incidents were reported in the past 12 months?
3.4: Are incident response playbooks regularly tested and updated?

0.0: Has the organization established, documented, and communicated an information security policy approved by management?
0.1: Are information security roles and responsibilities clearly defined and assigned?
0.2: Describe how the information security policy is reviewed and updated.
0.3: How frequently is the information security policy reviewed?

1.0: Is there a formal access control policy that defines user access rights based on business requirements?
1.1: Are user access rights reviewed periodically to ensure they remain appropriate?
1.2: Describe the process for provisioning, modifying, and revoking user access.
1.3: What is the frequency of access rights recertification for privileged accounts?
1.4: Are all authentication events logged and monitored?

2.0: Is there a cryptographic key management policy covering key generation, storage, distribution, and destruction?
2.1: Are cryptographic controls applied to protect sensitive information in accordance with legal and regulatory requirements?
2.2: Describe the encryption standards and algorithms used for data at rest and in transit.
2.3: Are cryptographic keys rotated according to a defined schedule?

3.0: Are information security requirements defined and agreed upon in supplier contracts?
3.1: Is there a process to monitor and review supplier compliance with information security obligations?
3.2: Describe the supplier risk assessment methodology applied to critical service providers.
3.3: How often are critical suppliers audited or reassessed for security compliance?
3.4: Are supplier-related security incidents tracked and reviewed?

0.0: Are logical and physical access controls implemented to prevent unauthorized access to system resources?
0.1: Is there a risk assessment process to identify and respond to security threats?
0.2: Describe the security monitoring and alerting mechanisms in place.
0.3: Are security incidents logged, investigated, and resolved in a timely manner?

1.0: Is system availability monitored and measured against defined service level objectives?
1.1: Are backup and recovery procedures documented and tested regularly?
1.2: Describe the organization's approach to capacity planning and resource scaling.
1.3: What is the target recovery time objective (RTO) for critical systems?
1.4: Have disaster recovery procedures been tested in the past 12 months?

2.0: Is confidential information classified and labeled according to its sensitivity?
2.1: Are encryption and access controls applied to protect confidential data throughout its lifecycle?
2.2: Describe the data retention and disposal procedures for confidential information.
2.3: Are employees required to sign confidentiality agreements?

3.0: Are automated controls in place to ensure data processing is complete, accurate, and timely?
3.1: Is there a process to detect and correct processing errors?
3.2: Describe the validation and reconciliation procedures used to verify data integrity.
3.3: Are processing outputs reviewed and approved before finalization?
3.4: What is the average error rate in critical data processing workflows?

0.0: Has the organization designated a Security Official responsible for developing and implementing security policies?
0.1: Is there a documented risk analysis process to assess potential risks to ePHI?
0.2: Describe the security awareness and training program for workforce members.
0.3: Are workforce members required to complete HIPAA security training annually?

1.0: Are physical access controls implemented to limit access to facilities where ePHI is stored or processed?
1.1: Is there a workstation security policy that defines acceptable use and physical safeguards?
1.2: Describe the device and media disposal procedures to ensure ePHI is securely destroyed.
1.3: Are visitor access logs maintained and reviewed?

2.0: Are unique user IDs assigned to track individual access to ePHI?
2.1: Is encryption applied to ePHI both at rest and in transit?
2.2: Describe the audit logging and monitoring mechanisms for ePHI access.
2.3: Are automatic logoff controls enabled after a defined period of inactivity?
2.4: What is the password complexity policy for accounts accessing ePHI?

3.0: Is there a documented breach notification process aligned with HIPAA requirements (60-day notification rule)?
3.1: Are workforce members trained to identify and report potential breaches of ePHI?
3.2: Describe the risk assessment methodology used to determine if a breach notification is required.
3.3: How many breach incidents were reported in the past 12 months?

0.0: Are all physical and software assets inventoried and maintained in an asset register?
0.1: Is there a business impact analysis (BIA) that identifies critical functions and dependencies?
0.2: Describe the risk assessment process used to identify and prioritize cybersecurity risks.
0.3: Are third-party dependencies and supply chain risks documented and monitored?

1.0: Are identity and access management controls implemented, including least privilege and role-based access?
1.1: Is there a data protection strategy covering encryption, classification, and secure disposal?
1.2: Describe the security awareness and training program for all personnel.
1.3: Are secure development practices integrated into the software development lifecycle?
1.4: What percentage of systems are protected by endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions?

2.0: Are security events continuously monitored and analyzed to detect anomalies?
2.1: Is there a Security Operations Center (SOC) or equivalent function responsible for detection and analysis?
2.2: Describe the threat intelligence sources and integration mechanisms used.
2.3: Are detection processes tested and validated periodically?

3.0: Is there a documented incident response plan that includes roles, procedures, and communication protocols?
3.1: Are incident response capabilities tested through tabletop exercises or simulations at least annually?
3.2: Describe the incident categorization and escalation process.
3.3: How often are lessons learned from incidents incorporated into response plans?

4.0: Is there a documented recovery plan that defines recovery objectives and procedures?
4.1: Are recovery plans tested at least annually to ensure effectiveness?
4.2: Describe the backup strategy and validation procedures.
4.3: Are improvements identified during recovery exercises tracked and implemented?

0.0: Is there an inventory of all authorized and unauthorized hardware assets maintained in real-time?
0.1: Is there an inventory of all authorized and unauthorized software assets maintained in real-time?
0.2: Describe the vulnerability scanning and patch management process.
0.3: What is the average time to deploy critical patches?
0.4: Are administrative privileges restricted and monitored?

1.0: Is multi-factor authentication (MFA) enforced for all remote access and privileged accounts?
1.1: Are network devices configured according to security benchmarks (e.g., CIS Benchmarks)?
1.2: Describe the security awareness training program and its frequency.
1.3: Are centralized log management and analysis tools deployed?
1.4: What percentage of endpoints have endpoint detection and response (EDR) deployed?

2.0: Is there a formal penetration testing program conducted by third parties at least annually?
2.1: Are security operations center (SOC) or managed detection and response (MDR) capabilities in place?
2.2: Describe the threat intelligence program and how it informs security decisions.
2.3: Are red team/blue team exercises conducted to test detection and response capabilities?
2.4: Is there a formal secure software development lifecycle (SDLC) with integrated security testing?

slaComplianceRate.label: SLA compliance rate
slaComplianceRate.description: Percentage of assessments completed on time

slaPoliciesPage.description: Manage automated SLA tracking and reminder policies
slaPoliciesPage.premiumRequired: SLA policies require a Premium plan. Upgrade to enable automated reminders and escalation.

Analytics.description: Operational insights and compliance analytics for your vendor risk program.
